intro: Bones are living organs. They are 'factories' that produce blood cells and store minerals.
notes: Red Bone Marrow: Makes Red Blood Cells.
Yellow Bone Marrow: Stores Fat & makes White Blood Cells.

hotspots.label: Red Bone Marrow
hotspots.description:
Found inside the honeycomb spaces of Spongy Bone. It is responsible for Haemopoiesis (making blood cells).

It produces Red Blood Cells, Platelets, and most White Blood Cells.
hotspots.examTip: Question: Where are red blood cells made? Answer: In the Red Bone Marrow.

hotspots.label: Yellow Bone Marrow
hotspots.description:
Found in the hollow center (medullary cavity) of long bones. It is yellow because it consists mainly of Fat cells.

In emergencies, it can convert back to red marrow to make blood.

hotspots.label: Articular Cartilage
hotspots.description:
A smooth, slippery white tissue that covers the ends of bones at joints.

It acts as a shock absorber and prevents bones from rubbing against each other.
hotspots.examTip: If cartilage wears out, it causes a painful condition called Arthritis.

hotspots.label: Spongy Bone
hotspots.description:
Also called Cancellous bone. It looks like a sponge with many holes.

It is lighter than compact bone and is usually found at the ends (heads) of long bones.
hotspots.examTip: It is porous to hold the Red Bone Marrow.

hotspots.label: Compact Bone
hotspots.description:
The dense, hard outer shell of the bone. It gives the bone its strength and white appearance.

It stores important minerals like Calcium and Phosphorus.
hotspots.examTip: It provides the structural strength to support body weight.
